narration: You just got a raise — congrats. But here's the wild thing: 80% of people end up poorer after a raise. It's called the raise trap.
visual: Person silhouette celebrating with a RAISE badge popping in, then dollar coins draining downward off screen. Dark background with green accent.

narration: It's called lifestyle creep, and it's savage. For every $100 raise you get, the average person spends $120 more. You're losing ground.
visual: Two vertical bar charts growing from a baseline — EARN bar reaches $100 in green, SPEND bar overshoots to $120 in red. Light background.

narration: Number one trigger is the car upgrade. New car searches jump 34% right after raises. That $400-a-month payment just ate your whole raise.
visual: SVG car rolling in from the right, a price tag showing $400/month pops in, then green dollar bills flow into the car and disappear.

narration: Number two is apartment envy. Moving somewhere nicer feels like a reward. But $500 more per month costs you $180,000 in wealth over 30 years.
visual: Small house on left, arrow pointing right, large fancy house on right. Cost counter ticks up to $180,000 in red. Light background.

narration: Number three: the invisible splurge. Restaurants, subscriptions, clothes — it all creeps up silently. Your raise is gone in 6 months with no big purchase.
visual: Piggy bank center with four lifestyle icons at corners: coffee cup, phone, shopping bag, fork. Month counter ticks 1 through 6 at bottom.

narration: Here's the fix. On raise day, auto-transfer the extra before your lifestyle adjusts. Bank 50% of every raise and start building real wealth today.
visual: Phone screen showing auto-transfer button, arrow sending money to a filling piggy bank. Bold CTA banner: THE 50% RULE. Light background.
